_meta.description: Persistent knowledge base for the AI Governance Monitor. Updated only when material changes occur. Never delete entries — archive closed episodes instead.
_meta.calibration: EU AI Act 2026 / GPAI Code of Practice applied 2026-04-02

module_7_risk_vectors.summary: EU/US governance schism: EP Omnibus vote (569–45–23) vs White House nonbinding framework explicitly opposing new federal AI regulator. Domestic US patchwork (Colorado, New York, Texas, Georgia, Minnesota) cannot yet be federally preempted. Labs have structural incentive to route EU compliance through US legal entities. Trump National Policy Framework (20 March 2026) recommends federal preemption of state AI laws — not yet enacted.

module_7_risk_vectors.summary: AI agent orchestration infrastructure (Langflow, LiteLLM, n8n) is established attack surface. CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS 9.3, Langflow) exploited within 20 hours of advisory — no public PoC required. CVE-2026-33634 (Trivy/LiteLLM supply chain) found in 36% of monitored cloud environments. CISA enforcement only applies to federal agencies; private sector exposure unaddressed. Grok 4 evaluated by AISI as capable of providing chemical/biological uplift to non-experts.

module_7_risk_vectors.summary: US Commerce Dept withdrew planned AI chip export rule (13 March 2026). No replacement rule published. Biden-era AI Diffusion Rule rescinded May 2025. H20 chip requires BIS export license indefinitely (April 2025, Nvidia filing). H200 approved for China with 50% of US domestic sales cap (January 2026, Trump administration). Bilateral supply lock-in intensifying; cloud IaaS providers can serve Chinese customers via foreign data centers without triggering hardware export controls.

module_9_eu_ai_act_tracker.standards_vacuum_trigger: CEN-CENELEC JTC21 harmonised standards still in draft; compliance obligations activate 2 August 2026 without technical safe harbour if Omnibus trilogue stalls

layers.name: AI Act Text
layers.note: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 in force. General application 2 August 2026.

layers.name: Delegated / Implementing Acts
layers.note: Commission drafting. Article 6 high-risk classification guidelines published February 2026. GPAI classification criteria and high-risk system list updates pending.

layers.name: Harmonised Standards (CEN-CENELEC JTC21)
layers.note: JTC21 missed April 2025 delivery deadline. CEN-CENELEC Boards adopted exceptional package Oct 14-16 2025 to accelerate delivery targeting Q4 2026 — after Aug 2 deadline. Until OJ citation, presumption of conformity unavailable for high-risk AI. Standards will not provide pre-August safe harbour.

layers.name: GPAI Code of Practice
layers.status: Final published — Commission adequacy assessment pending
layers.note: Final GPAI CoP published July 10, 2025 (digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu). Three chapters: Transparency, Copyright, Safety and Security. Commission and AI Board confirmed it is an adequate voluntary tool. Not yet adopted as implementing act. Enforcement of GPAI obligations begins August 2, 2026.

layers.name: National Enforcement (NCAs)
layers.note: Member states designation slower than expected — may affect early enforcement consistency. Most not yet fully operational for AI Act enforcement. Track NCA enforcement capacity monthly.

layers.name: AI Office Supervisory Decisions
layers.note: No formal supervisory decisions issued. AI Office operational but pre-enforcement. First supervisory decision = landmark M09+M10 item. Full enforcement authority for GPAI models from August 2, 2026.

layers.name: Digital Omnibus Trilogue
layers.note: EP adopted position 26 March (569–45–23). Council position adopted 13 March. Second political trilogue scheduled April 28, 2026 — political agreement expected at this meeting per A&O Shearman (April 9) and Ropes Gray (April 24). If agreement reached: Parliament vote May, Council June, OJ July, entry into force 1 August. Convergence: fixed high-risk dates Dec 2, 2027 (Annex III) and Aug 2, 2028 (Annex I) both institutions agree. New prohibitions on nudifier AI and CSAM tools agreed by both. Divergence: AI literacy binding vs soft, synthetic content timing (Nov 2026 Parliament vs Feb 2027 Council), sectoral integration.

law_highlights.title: GPAI Code of Practice — Final Publication July 10, 2025
law_highlights.summary: Final GPAI CoP published at digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu. Three chapters covering Transparency, Copyright, Safety and Security. Commission and AI Board confirmed it is an adequate voluntary tool for GPAI model providers to demonstrate AI Act compliance. Enforcement of GPAI obligations begins August 2, 2026.

law_highlights.title: CEN-CENELEC JTC21 Exceptional Acceleration Package — October 2025
law_highlights.summary: CEN-CENELEC Boards adopted exceptional package Oct 14-16 2025 to accelerate AI harmonised standards targeting Q4 2026 delivery. Allows direct publication after positive Enquiry vote, skipping Formal Vote. Standards still expected after August 2, 2026 deadline — no safe harbour available before high-risk obligations apply.

law_highlights.title: Article 6 High-Risk Classification Guidelines — February 2026
law_highlights.summary: European Commission published guidelines specifying practical implementation of Article 6 high-risk AI classification in February 2026, meeting the February 2 2026 deadline.

module_15_aisi_pipeline.pipeline_status: No confirmed senior permanent appointments — structural leadership vacuum persists approaching August 2026 enforcement deadline
module_15_aisi_pipeline.institutional_note: UK AISI: Adam Beaumont (ex-GCHQ Chief AI Officer) confirmed as Interim Director October 2025 — replacing Oliver Ilott (promoted to interim DG AI at DSIT). No permanent director appointment confirmed as of April 28, 2026. US NIST CAISI: active with MOU with GSA (March 2026) and CRADA with OpenMined (March 2026); AI Agent Standards Initiative launched February 2026. EU AI Office: no new senior appointments; full enforcement authority from August 2, 2026.

module_5_standards_vacuum.detail: CEN-CENELEC JTC21 harmonised standards still in draft. AI Act general application 2 August 2026. Exceptional acceleration package adopted October 2025 targeting Q4 2026 delivery — still after August 2 deadline. High-risk AI providers face compliance obligations without harmonised technical safe harbour regardless of Omnibus outcome for the August 2 window.
module_5_standards_vacuum.omnibus_mitigation: If trilogue agrees text before 2 Aug 2026, Annex III high-risk rules delayed to Dec 2027. Watermarking obligations activate Nov 2026 (Parliament) or Feb 2027 (Council) regardless. April 28 political meeting underway — result unknown at time of writing.

cen_cenelec_acceleration.description: CEN-CENELEC Boards Oct 14-16 2025 adopted exceptional package: direct publication after positive Enquiry vote (skipping Formal Vote); small drafting group to finalize 6 most-delayed drafts
cen_cenelec_acceleration.implications: Q4 2026 delivery is after August 2 deadline — vacuum persists for high-risk obligations. Standards will cover compliance pathway only for prospective post-August 2026 market entries.

module_5_ciyuan.detail: Liu Liehong (National Data Administration) adopted 'ciyuan' (词元) as official Mandarin for AI token at China Development Forum (24 March 2026), framing tokens as 'settlement unit of the intelligent era'. Daily token usage exceeded 140 trillion in March 2026. Alibaba launched Token Hub subsidiary. China National Committee for Terminology recommending ciyuan as standardised term.

flags.title: AI-enabled FIMI: capability layer update — agentic autonomous narrative attacks confirmed
flags.linkage: EEAS 4th FIMI Report (10 March) and Blackbird.AI RAV3N confirm the transition from AI-assisted to AI-autonomous narrative attack operations. The AI Governance Monitor's capability layer (agentic AI autonomous breach documented by Anthropic, Sep 2025; applied to narrative operations Feb 2026) directly feeds the FIMI monitor's actor and campaign tracking.
flags.ai_governance_perspective: The capability collapse in narrative attack costs is a direct output of frontier model advances we track in M02 and M12. The FIMI monitor provides attribution and campaign-level context; we provide the underlying capability trajectory that determines how fast those campaigns will evolve.

flags.title: AI deepfakes in 2026 US midterms + Stanford Internet Observatory dismantlement: dual democratic integrity stress
flags.linkage: The AI deepfake capability documented in M12 is now confirmed as active in US midterm campaigns (Reuters, 28 March). Simultaneously, the Stanford Internet Observatory — the primary independent monitoring infrastructure for US election disinformation — was dismantled under political pressure. The AI Governance Monitor tracks both the capability (deepfakes at scale) and the governance vacuum (no disclosure law, SIO gone); the Democratic Integrity monitor tracks the electoral environment they are being deployed into.
flags.ai_governance_perspective: The SIO dismantlement is itself a governance failure — defunding the primary academic monitoring body that would detect AI-enabled election interference removes early-warning capacity just as the capability is deployed.

layer_5_nca.note: Designation slower than expected; enforcement capacity variable across member states. Track NCA enforcement capacity monthly.

layer_6_ai_office.note: No enforcement decisions yet. Full enforcement authority from August 2, 2026. First supervisory decision = landmark M09+M10 item.

Fixed high-risk dates (Dec 2027/Aug 2028) agreed by both institutions. New NCII/CSAM prohibitions agreed. Remaining divergence: literacy obligation, synthetic content timing, sectoral integration.

uk.name: AI Security Institute (rebranded from AISI, early 2025)
uk.note: Adam Beaumont confirmed as Interim Director (October 2025) — ex-GCHQ Chief AI Officer. No permanent director. Mandate shift: safety evaluation → security-focused oversight. Conducted Grok 4 evaluation (CBRN uplift confirmed). Track staff changes — departures/appointments carry higher significance in unstable institutional period.

us.name: NIST CAISI (Center for AI Standards and Innovation) — rebranded from AI Safety Institute
us.note: Active in 2026: AI Agent Standards Initiative (Feb 17), MOU with GSA for USAi federal procurement (Mar 18), CRADA with OpenMined (Mar 27). Trump deregulatory pivot reduced enforcement mandate vs Biden era. NIST AI RMF 1.0 in effect but lacks executive enforcement mandate.

eu.name: EU AI Office
eu.note: Operational, no binding enforcement decisions yet. Full enforcement authority from August 2, 2026. First supervisory decision = landmark M09+M10 item. GPAI obligations have been enforceable since August 2025; enforcement actions begin August 2026.

gaps_register.finding: FTC AI enforcement April 2026 — three enforcement tracks (Section 5 deception, automated decision disclosure, AI-generated content deception) active with consent orders. Source: aipolicydesk.com April 22 2026 and morganlewis.com April 2 2026. Specific named consent orders not individually verified at FTC.gov primary source in this pass.
gaps_register.action_required: Verify individual FTC consent orders at ftc.gov primary source; add to windowed_events if module_9 scope extended to US enforcement, or add Regulatory Fragmentation vector footnote

gaps_register.finding: Google DeepMind Frontier Safety Framework v3.0 confirmed September 22, 2025. Third FSF version adds harmful manipulation risk, deceptive alignment risk, shutdown resistance protocols. Source: Facebook/social report confirms FSF 3.0 Sept 22 2025; Google AI Responsibility Update 2026 PDF (ai.google) confirms FSF central to 2026 approach. Not yet in module_2 (lab registry) as no module_2 present in current state.
gaps_register.suggested_module: module_2 (lab registry) — not present in current state; added to ongoing_lab_postures as new entry instead
gaps_register.action_required: Consider adding module_2 lab registry module in next operator review if scope expansion warranted; Google DeepMind FSF v3.0 is material structural fact

gaps_register.finding: Illinois AI legislation: SB 3444 (OpenAI-backed — liability shield) and SB 3261 (Anthropic-backed — incident reporting + child safety) both active in April 2026. Illustrates lab-backed divergence in US state AI liability frameworks. Source: Fortune April 17, Quartz April 10.
gaps_register.suggested_module: module_7_risk_vectors (Governance Fragmentation windowed events) or country_grid_status (USA)
gaps_register.action_required: Consider adding Illinois bills as windowed event in Governance Fragmentation vector or USA country grid note. Partially incorporated in USA country_grid_status binding_law field in this pass.

gaps_register.finding: Musk v. Altman trial commenced April 28, 2026 in Oakland federal court. Jury selection started; opening statements expected April 29. Trial through mid-May. Jury advisory, final ruling by Judge Gonzalez Rogers. Seeks $134B damages + reversal of OpenAI for-profit conversion. Key witnesses: Musk, Altman, Satya Nadella, Mira Murati (former OpenAI COO).
gaps_register.suggested_module: module_7_risk_vectors (Platform Power windowed event) — high importance for OpenAI governance structural facts
gaps_register.action_required: Add as windowed event to Platform Power vector. Outcome potentially material to concentration_index_2026 (OpenAI governance structure). Monitor through May 2026. Partially incorporated in ongoing_lab_postures OpenAI entry in this pass.

gaps_register.finding: UK copyright reform deferred indefinitely (March 19, 2026). Government published report and impact assessment but concluded 'no longer has a preferred option' for legislative reform on AI training — further consultation period opened. Creates regulatory uncertainty for UK AI training data governance.
gaps_register.action_required: Add to UK country_grid_status as copyright uncertainty note. Important for AGM's litigation/enforcement windowed events when court cases proceed on existing law.
